meaning: vi.打滚,<喻>沉迷,颠簸n.打滚,泥坑,堕落
examples: We do fairly wallow in the mud, sir, saving your worship's presence.
He loves to wallow in a hot bath after a game.
She's not someone who likes to wallow in self-pity.
"Europe is going to continue to wallow in misery for the foreseeable future, " Mr. Steinmetz said.
The stickiest remaining mud is the patch in which the pilots like to wallow.
